15	//----------------------------------------------------------------------------
16	// Package: coralnpu_env_pkg
17	// Description: Package for the CoralNPU UVM Environment.
18	//----------------------------------------------------------------------------
19	package coralnpu_env_pkg;
20	
21	  import uvm_pkg::*;
22	  `include "uvm_macros.svh"
23	
24	  // Import agent packages
25	  import coralnpu_axi_master_agent_pkg::*;
26	  import coralnpu_axi_slave_agent_pkg::*;
27	  import coralnpu_irq_agent_pkg::*;
28	  import coralnpu_rvvi_agent_pkg::*;
29	  import coralnpu_cosim_checker_pkg::*;
30	
31	  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
32	  // Class: coralnpu_env
33	  //--------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	  class coralnpu_env extends uvm_env;
35	    `uvm_component_utils(coralnpu_env)
36	
37	    // Agent and Checker Handles
38	    coralnpu_axi_master_agent m_master_agent; // Drives DUT Slave Port
39	    coralnpu_axi_slave_agent  m_slave_agent;  // Responds to DUT Master Port
40	    coralnpu_irq_agent        m_irq_agent;    // Drives IRQ/Control Signals
41	    coralnpu_rvvi_agent       m_rvvi_agent;   // Passive agent for RVVI
42	    coralnpu_cosim_checker    m_cosim_checker; // Manages co-simulation against MPACT simulator
43	
44	    // Constructor
45	    function new(string name = "coralnpu_env", uvm_component parent = null);
46	      super.new(name, parent);
47	    endfunction : new
48	
49	    // Build phase: Create agent instances
50	    virtual function void build_phase(uvm_phase phase);
51	      super.build_phase(phase);
52	      `uvm_info(get_type_name(), "Build phase starting", UVM_MEDIUM)
53	
54	      // Create the components
55	      m_master_agent = coralnpu_axi_master_agent::type_id::create("m_master_agent", this);
56	      m_slave_agent  = coralnpu_axi_slave_agent::type_id::create("m_slave_agent", this);
57	      m_irq_agent    = coralnpu_irq_agent::type_id::create("m_irq_agent", this);
58	      m_rvvi_agent   = coralnpu_rvvi_agent::type_id::create("m_rvvi_agent", this);
59	      m_cosim_checker = coralnpu_cosim_checker::type_id::create("m_cosim_checker", this);
60	
61	      `uvm_info(get_type_name(), "Build phase finished", UVM_MEDIUM)
62	    endfunction : build_phase
63	
64	    // Connect phase: Connect components if needed (e.g., monitors later)
65	    virtual function void connect_phase(uvm_phase phase);
66	      super.connect_phase(phase);
67	    endfunction : connect_phase
68	
69	  endclass : coralnpu_env
70	
71	endpackage : coralnpu_env_pkg

8	// clock & reset -----------------------------------------------------
9	  logic clk;
10	  logic rst_n;

13	  parameter sim_cycle = 10;
14

18	  always

21	    end
22	
23	  //Driver reset depending on rst_delay

25	      clk = 0;
26	      rst_n = 0;
27	      repeat (rst_delay) @(posedge clk);
28	      rst_n = 1'b1;
29	      @(clk);
30	  end

file.content:
name: CoralNPU RTL Verilator Simulation
on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  pull_request:
  push:
    tags:
      - "*"
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-verilator:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    permissions:
      contents: write
    timeout-minutes: 30
    env:
      CI_BUILD_STAGE_NAME: build
      CI_RUNS_ON: ubuntu-24.04
      CI_OS_NAME: linux
      CI_VERILATOR_INSTALL_DIR: ${{ github.workspace }}/verilator_install
    steps:
      - name: clone verilator
        run: |
          git clone https://github.com/verilator/verilator
      - name: install dependencies
        run: ${{ github.workspace }}/verilator/ci/ci-install.bash
      - name:
        run: |
          pushd ${{ github.workspace }}/verilator
          autoconf
          ./configure --prefix="${{ env.CI_VERILATOR_INSTALL_DIR }}"
          make -j $(nproc)
          make install
          popd
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: verilator
          path: |
            ${{ env.CI_VERILATOR_INSTALL_DIR }}
  coralnpu-sim-verilator:
    runs-on: ubuntu-24.04
    permissions:
      contents: write
    timeout-minutes: 60
    needs:
      - build-verilator
    env:
      CI_BUILD_STAGE_NAME: build
      CI_RUNS_ON: ubuntu-24.04
      CI_OS_NAME: linux
      CI_TMPDIR: /tmp
      CI_CORALNPU_MPACT_COMMIT: 61a6317aca4de62a4862181d24dc22a1795bda43
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
          ssh-key: ${{ secrets.ACTIONS_DEPLOY_KEY }}
      - uses: bazel-contrib/setup-bazel@0.15.0
        with:
          # Avoid downloading Bazel every time.
          bazelisk-cache: true
          # Store build cache per workflow.
          disk-cache: ${{ github.workflow }}
          # Share repository cache between workflows.
          repository-cache: true
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v8
        with:
          name: verilator
          path: ${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/verilator_install
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update
          sudo apt-get install -y git gcc autoconf bc bison build-essential ccache clang curl device-tree-compiler flex fuse3 gawk git mold python-is-python3 python3 python3-full python3-pip python3-pyelftools python3-setuptools python3-venv srecord unzip xxd zip openjdk-17-jre z3
      - name: Clone UVM-1.2
        run: |
          git clone https://github.com/chipsalliance/uvm-verilator.git -b uvm-1.2 ${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/uvm-verilator
          git -C ${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/uvm-verilator cherry-pick -n --strategy=recursive -X theirs 5a37baacfed0722b523b05decc9b94fe3e9efbe4
      - name: Clone CoralNPU-mpact
        run: |
          git clone https://github.com/google-coral/coralnpu-mpact.git ${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/coralnpu-mpact
          git -C ${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/coralnpu-mpact checkout ${{ env.CI_CORALNPU_MPACT_COMMIT }}
      - name: Generate RTL and run simulation
        run: |
          chmod +x -R ${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/verilator_install/bin
          export PATH=$PATH:${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/verilator_install/bin
          export UVM=${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/uvm-verilator
          export CORALNPU_MPACT=${{ env.CI_TMPDIR }}/coralnpu-mpact
          bazel build //hdl/chisel/src/coralnpu:rvv_core_mini_axi_cc_library_emit_verilog
          python3 utils/run_uvm_regression.py --simulator=verilator
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: uvm_regression_result.zip
          path: |
            ${{ github.workspace }}/uvm_regression_*.zip

content.thinking: This is very useful. There's a `utils/run_uvm_regression.py` script that runs the UVM regression. Let me read it to understand the full flow, since it's the key driver of the regression. This is critical for the practice task and understanding how the regress lists are consumed.

113	    "//examples:coralnpu_v2_rvv_add_intrinsic": 200000,

119	    (0x10000, 0x8000),  # DTCM
120	    (0x20000000, 0x400000),  # DRAM
121	]

125	def get_spike_memory_map_str() -> str:
126	
127	    return ",".join([
128	        f"0x{start:x}:0x{length:x}" for start, length in SPIKE_MEMORY_REGIONS
129	    ])

131	
132	def get_targets(limit: Optional[int] = None,
133	                target: Optional[str] = None) -> List[str]:
134	
135	    if target:
136	        return [target]
137	
138	    logging.info("Querying bazel targets...")
139	    # Using --output=xml to parse attributes
140	    cmd = ["bazel", "query", "kind(coralnpu_v2_binary, //...)", "--output=xml"]
141	    try:
142	        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
143	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
144	        logging.error(f"Bazel query failed: {e}")
145	        if e.stderr:
146	            logging.error(f"Stderr: {e.stderr}")
147	        sys.exit(1)
148	    root = ET.fromstring(result.stdout)
149	    targets = []
150	    for rule in root.findall('rule'):
151	        target_name_full = rule.attrib['name']  # //package:target
152	
153	        # Check against DENYLIST
154	        if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(target_name_full, pattern) for pattern in DENYLIST):
155	            continue
156	
157	        # Check linker_script attribute
158	        linker_script_elem = rule.find("label[@name='linker_script']")
159	        if linker_script_elem is not None:
160	            linker_script = linker_script_elem.attrib['value']
161	
162	            # Extract package and name from target
163	            # e.g. //tests/cocotb:align_test
164	            if ':' in target_name_full:
165	                parts = target_name_full.split(':')
166	                pkg = parts[0]
167	                name = parts[1]
168	            else:
169	                # Handling edge case //package (implicit :package)
170	                pkg = target_name_full
171	                name = target_name_full.split('/')[-1]
172	
173	            # Construct expected default linker script
174	            # Default is generated in the same package with name <target_name>.ld
175	            expected_linker_script = f"{pkg}:{name}.ld"
176	
177	            if linker_script == expected_linker_script:
178	                targets.append(target_name_full)
179	            else:
180	                # Skipping targets with custom/non-default linker scripts
181	                pass
182	        else:
183	            # If no linker_script attribute is found, it might not be a valid target for us, skip.
184	            pass
185	
186	    targets = sorted(targets)
187	    if limit:
188	        return targets[:limit]
189	    return targets
190	
191	
192	def build_targets(targets: List[str]) -> bool:
193	    logging.info(f"Building {len(targets)} targets...")
194	    # Split into chunks to avoid command line length limits if necessary,
195	    # but for now pass all. Bazel handles many targets well.
196	    cmd = ["bazel", "build"] + targets
197	    try:
198	        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
199	        return True
200	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
201	        logging.error(f"Build failed for some targets: {e}")
202	        return False
203	
204	
205	def get_elf_source_path(target: str) -> Optional[str]:
206	    # Use bazel cquery to get the actual output path
207	    cmd = ["bazel", "cquery", "--output=files", target]
208	    try:
209	        result = subprocess.run(cmd,
210	                                capture_output=True,
211	                                text=True,
212	                                check=True)
213	        lines = result.stdout.strip().split('\n')
214	        for line in lines:
215	            if line.endswith('.elf'):
216	                return line.strip()
217	        return None
218	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
219	        logging.error(f"Error finding ELF source path: {e}")
220	        return None
221	
222	
223	def get_entry_point(elf_path: str) -> int:
224	    try:
225	        with open(elf_path, 'rb') as f:
226	            elf = ELFFile(f)
227	            return elf.header.e_entry
228	    except Exception as e:
229	        logging.error(f"Error reading ELF entry point: {e}")
230	        return 0
231	
232	
233	def get_tohost_addr(elf_path: str) -> Optional[int]:
234	    try:
235	        with open(elf_path, 'rb') as f:
236	            elf = ELFFile(f)
237	            symtab = elf.get_section_by_name('.symtab')
238	            if symtab:
239	                syms = symtab.get_symbol_by_name('tohost')
240	                if syms and len(syms) > 0:
241	                    return syms[0]['st_value']
242	    except Exception as e:
243	        logging.error(f"Error reading tohost addr: {e}")
244	    return None
245	
246	def build_simulator(mpact_root: str,
247	                    simulator: str,
248	                    mpact_riscv_root: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
249	    logging.info("Building UVM Simulator (simv)...")
250	    env = os.environ.copy()
251	    env["CORALNPU_MPACT"] = mpact_root
252	    if mpact_riscv_root:
253	        env["CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV"] = mpact_riscv_root
254	
255	    cmd = ["make", "-C", "tests/uvm", "compile", f"SIMULATOR={simulator}"]
256	    try:
257	        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, env=env)
258	        return True
259	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
260	        logging.error(f"Simulator build failed: {e}")
261	        return False
262	
263	
264	def build_spike() -> Optional[str]:
265	    logging.info("Building Spike Simulator...")
266	    cmd = ["bazel", "build", "@riscv_isa_sim//:riscv_isa_sim"]
267	    try:
268	        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
269	        # Return the absolute path to the binary
270	        return os.path.abspath(
271	            "bazel-bin/external/riscv_isa_sim/riscv_isa_sim/bin/spike")
272	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
273	        logging.error(f"Spike build failed: {e}")
274	        return None
275	
276	
277	def generate_spike_log(spike_bin: str,
278	                       elf_path: str,
279	                       log_path: str,
280	                       entry_point: int = 0,
281	                       timeout: int = 30) -> bool:
282	
283	    logging.info(
284	        f"Generating Spike log for {elf_path} (Entry: 0x{entry_point:x})...")
285	    cmd = [
286	        spike_bin, f"-m{get_spike_memory_map_str()}", f"--isa={SPIKE_ISA}",
287	        "--misaligned", "-l", "--log-commits", f"--pc={entry_point}", elf_path
288	    ]
289	    try:
290	        with open(log_path, 'w') as f:
291	            process = subprocess.Popen(cmd,
292	                                       stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
293	                                       stdout=f,
294	                                       stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
295	                                       start_new_session=True)
296	            try:
297	                process.wait(timeout=timeout)
298	            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
299	                logging.warning(f"Spike timed out (PID: {process.pid})")
300	                os.killpg(os.getpgid(process.pid), signal.SIGTERM)
301	                process.wait(timeout=5)
302	                return False
303	
304	            if process.returncode != 0:
305	                logging.error(
306	                    f"Spike failed with exit code {process.returncode}")
307	                return False
308	        return True
309	    except Exception as e:
310	        logging.error(f"Spike generation failed: {e}")
311	        return False
312	
313	
314	def run_uvm(elf_path: str,
315	            spike_log_path: Optional[str] = None,
316	            target: Optional[str] = None,
317	            mpact_root: Optional[str] = None,
318	            tohost_addr: Optional[int] = None) -> Tuple[str, str, str]:
319	
320	    logging.info(f"Running UVM for {elf_path}...")
321	    if not mpact_root:
322	        mpact_root = resolve_default_mpact_root()
323	    if not os.path.exists(elf_path):
324	        # We don't want to deal too much with handling exceptions in
325	        # the run loop that calls this. We'll just log in the CSV and let
326	        # the user pick up the pieces.
327	        return "BUILD_ARTIFACT_MISSING", "ELF file not found", ""
328	
329	    env = os.environ.copy()
330	    env["CORALNPU_MPACT"] = mpact_root
331	    # Use absolute path for TEST_ELF
332	    abs_elf_path = os.path.abspath(elf_path)
333	    cmd = [
334	        "make", "-C", "tests/uvm", "run", "UVM_VERBOSITY=UVM_HIGH",
335	        f"TEST_ELF={abs_elf_path}"
336	    ]
337	
338	    if target and target in TIMEOUT_MAP:
339	        timeout_ns = TIMEOUT_MAP[target]
340	        cmd.append(f"TEST_TIMEOUT_NS={timeout_ns}")
341	        logging.info(f"  Using custom timeout: {timeout_ns} ns")
342	
343	
344	    if tohost_addr is not None:
345	        cmd.append(f"EXTRA_PLUSARGS=+TOHOST_ADDR={tohost_addr:08x}")
346	
347	    if spike_log_path:
348	        cmd.append(f"SPIKE_LOG={os.path.abspath(spike_log_path)}")
349	
350	    max_retries = 3
351	    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 1):
352	        try:
353	            result = subprocess.run(cmd,
354	                                    capture_output=True,
355	                                    text=True,
356	                                    env=env)
357	            output = result.stdout + result.stderr
358	            if result.returncode != 0 and "1-800-VERILOG" in output:
359	                if attempt < max_retries:
360	                    logging.warning(
361	                        f"  WARNING: License failure detected (1-800-VERILOG), retrying... (Attempt {attempt}/{max_retries})"
362	                    )
363	                    continue
364	                else:
365	                    logging.error(
366	                        f"  ERROR: License failure detected (1-800-VERILOG), max retries reached."
367	                    )
368	            # If successful or failed for other reasons, break loop
369	            break
370	
371	        except Exception as e:
372	            return "EXEC_FAIL", str(e), ""
373	
374	    # Process result (from last attempt)
375	    try:
376	        # Check for UVM errors/fatals regardless of return code
377	        # Match lines like: UVM_ERROR file.sv(123) @ 100: ... or UVM_FATAL @ 100: ...
378	        # Exclude summary lines like "UVM_ERROR : 0" or "Number of ... : 0"
379	        uvm_err = re.search(r"^\s*(UVM_(?:FATAL|ERROR)(?!.*:\s+0\s*$).*)$",
380	                            output, re.MULTILINE)
381	
382	        if result.returncode != 0:
383	            status = "FAIL"
384	            reason = "Unknown Error"
385	
386	            if uvm_err:
387	                reason = uvm_err.group(1).strip()
388	            elif "AXI_DECERR" in output:
389	                reason = "AXI_DECERR detected"
390	            else:
391	                lines = output.strip().split('\n')
392	                reason = "Make failed: " + (lines[-1] if lines else "Unknown")
393	
394	            # Sanitize reason for CSV (remove commas and newlines)
395	            reason = reason.replace(',', ';').replace('\n', ' ')
396	            return status, reason, output
397	
398	        # Even if return code is 0, check for UVM errors
399	        if uvm_err:
400	            reason = uvm_err.group(1).strip()
401	            # Sanitize reason for CSV
402	            reason = reason.replace(',', ';').replace('\n', ' ')
403	            return "FAIL", reason, output
404	
405	        status = "PASS"
406	        return status, "None", output
407	
408	    except Exception as e:
409	        return "EXEC_FAIL", str(e), ""
410	
411	
412	def get_riscv_test_artifacts() -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
413	    logging.info("Collecting riscv-tests artifacts...")
414	    target = "//third_party/riscv-tests:all_files"
415	    cmd = ["bazel", "cquery", target, "--output=files"]
416	    try:
417	        result = subprocess.run(cmd,
418	                                capture_output=True,
419	                                text=True,
420	                                check=True)
421	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
422	        logging.error(f"Failed to query riscv-tests outputs: {e}")
423	        return []
424	
425	    artifacts = []
426	    dirs = result.stdout.strip().split('\n')
427	    for d in dirs:
428	        d = d.strip()
429	        if not os.path.isdir(d):
430	            continue
431	
432	        for root, _, files in os.walk(d):
433	            for f in files:
434	                if f.endswith('.dump'):
435	                    continue
436	                # heuristic: starts with rv32
437	                if f.startswith('rv32'):
438	                    full_path = os.path.join(root, f)
439	                    # Construct a pseudo-target name
440	                    # e.g. //third_party/riscv-tests:rv32ui-p-add
441	                    name = f
442	                    pseudo_target = f"//third_party/riscv-tests:{name}"
443	                    if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(pseudo_target, pattern) for pattern in DENYLIST):
444	                        continue
445	                    artifacts.append((pseudo_target, full_path))
446	
447	    return sorted(artifacts)
448	
449	
450	def parse_arguments():
451	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run UVM regression")
452	    parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, help="Limit number of tests")
453	    parser.add_argument("--target", type=str, help="Run a single target")
454	    parser.add_argument("--simulator",
455	                        type=str,
456	                        help="Defines simulator used for tests",
457	                        default="vcs")
458	    parser.add_argument("--list-targets",
459	                        action="store_true",
460	                        help="List targets and exit")
461	    parser.add_argument("--check-spike-timeouts",
462	                        action="store_true",
463	                        help="Run only Spike generation to identify timeouts")
464	    parser.add_argument("--skip-riscv-tests",
465	                        action="store_true",
466	                        help="Skip riscv-tests")
467	    parser.add_argument(
468	        "--mpact-root",
469	        type=str,
470	        help="Path to MPACT root directory (overrides CORALNPU_MPACT env var)")
471	    parser.add_argument(
472	        "--mpact-riscv-root",
473	        type=str,
474	        help=
475	        "Path to MPACT RISCV directory (overrides CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV env var)"
476	    )
477	    parser.add_argument("--mpact-commit",
478	                        type=str,
479	                        help="Git commit hash to checkout for MPACT root")
480	    parser.add_argument(
481	        "--mpact-riscv-commit",
482	        type=str,
483	        help="Git commit hash to checkout for MPACT RISCV root")
484	    return parser.parse_args()
485	
486	
487	def checkout_git_commit(repo_root: str, commit: str):
488	    logging.info(f"Checking out {repo_root} at commit {commit}...")
489	    if not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(repo_root, ".git")):
490	        logging.error(
491	            f"{repo_root} is not a git repository. Cannot checkout commit.")
492	        sys.exit(1)
493	
494	    try:
495	        # Fetch first to ensure we have the commit
496	        subprocess.run(["git", "-C", repo_root, "fetch"], check=True)
497	        subprocess.run(["git", "-C", repo_root, "checkout", commit],
498	                       check=True)
499	    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
500	        logging.error(
501	            f"Failed to checkout commit {commit} in {repo_root}: {e}")
502	        sys.exit(1)
503	
504	
505	def resolve_default_mpact_root() -> str:
506	    # 1. Check if env var is already set
507	    if "CORALNPU_MPACT" in os.environ:
508	        return os.environ["CORALNPU_MPACT"]
509	
510	    # 2. If not set, dynamically resolve using Bazel
511	    try:
512	        logging.info("Dynamically resolving default @coralnpu_mpact path via Bazel...")
513	        # Force Bazel to fetch the repository if needed
514	        bazel_cache = os.environ.get("BAZEL_CACHE")
515	        fetch_cmd = ["bazel", "build"]
516	        if bazel_cache:
517	            fetch_cmd.append(f"--repository_cache={bazel_cache}")
518	        fetch_cmd.extend(["@coralnpu_mpact//:coralnpu_sim_users"])
519	
520	        # Run the build to force fetch
521	        subprocess.check_output(fetch_cmd, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)
522	
523	        # Query bazel info output_base to get the dynamic cache location
524	        output_base = subprocess.check_output(["bazel", "info", "output_base"]).decode("utf-8").strip()
525	        resolved_path = os.path.join(output_base, "external", "coralnpu_mpact")
526	        logging.info(f"Resolved default @coralnpu_mpact path: {resolved_path}")
527	        return resolved_path
528	    except Exception as e:
529	        logging.critical(f"Failed to dynamically resolve @coralnpu_mpact path via Bazel: {e}.")
530	        sys.exit(1)
531	
532	
533	def get_mpact_configs(args) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]:
534	    if args.mpact_root:
535	        mpact_root = os.path.abspath(args.mpact_root)
536	    else:
537	        mpact_root = resolve_default_mpact_root()
538	
539	    mpact_riscv_root = None
540	    if args.mpact_riscv_root:
541	        mpact_riscv_root = os.path.abspath(args.mpact_riscv_root)
542	    elif "CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV" in os.environ:
543	        mpact_riscv_root = os.environ["CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV"]
544	
545	    return mpact_root, mpact_riscv_root
546	
547	
548	def prepare_tests(args, standard_targets: List[str]) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
549	    # Build targets
550	    # Filter out pseudo-targets from standard_targets if they match riscv-tests pattern
551	    real_targets = [
552	        t for t in standard_targets
553	        if not t.startswith("//third_party/riscv-tests:")
554	    ]
555	    targets_to_build = real_targets
556	    if not args.skip_riscv_tests:
557	        targets_to_build.append("//third_party/riscv-tests:all_files")
558	
559	    if not build_targets(targets_to_build):
560	        logging.warning(
561	            "WARNING: Some targets failed to build. Continuing with available artifacts."
562	        )
563	
564	    # Now populate tests_to_run with valid ELFs
565	    tests_to_run = []
566	
567	    # 1. RISC-V Tests
568	    if not args.skip_riscv_tests:
569	        riscv_tests = get_riscv_test_artifacts()
570	        for t, elf in riscv_tests:
571	            if args.target and args.target != t:
572	                continue
573	            tests_to_run.append((t, elf))
574	
575	    # 2. Standard Targets
576	    if standard_targets:
577	        logging.info("Resolving standard target artifacts...")
578	    for t in standard_targets:
579	        if args.target and args.target != t:
580	            continue
581	        elf = get_elf_source_path(t)
582	        if elf:
583	            tests_to_run.append((t, elf))
584	
585	    # Apply global limit if set
586	    if args.limit:
587	        tests_to_run = tests_to_run[:args.limit]
588	
589	    return tests_to_run
590	
591	
592	def run_spike_timeout_check(tests_to_run: List[Tuple[str, str]],
593	                            spike_bin: str, temp_elf_dir: str):
594	    logging.info("--- Checking Spike Timeouts ---")
595	    failed_targets = []
596	    for i, (target, src_elf) in enumerate(tests_to_run):
597	        logging.info(f"[{i+1}/{len(tests_to_run)}] Checking {target}")
598	
599	        if src_elf and os.path.exists(src_elf):
600	            safe_name = target.replace('//', '').replace(':', '_').replace(
601	                '/', '_') + ".elf"
602	            dest_elf = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir, safe_name)
603	            try:
604	                if os.path.exists(dest_elf): os.remove(dest_elf)
605	                shutil.copy2(src_elf, dest_elf)
606	                # Permissions: 755 / -rwxr-xr-x / u=rwx,go=rx
607	                os.chmod(
608	                    dest_elf, stat.S_IRUSR | stat.S_IWUSR | stat.S_IXUSR
609	                    | stat.S_IRGRP
610	                    | stat.S_IXGRP | stat.S_IROTH | stat.S_IXOTH)
611	
612	                entry_point = get_entry_point(dest_elf)
613	                temp_spike_log = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir,
614	                                              safe_name + ".spike.log")
615	
616	                if not generate_spike_log(
617	                        spike_bin,
618	                        dest_elf,
619	                        temp_spike_log,
620	                        entry_point,
621	                        timeout=10):  # Short timeout for check
622	                    logging.error(f"  FAIL: {target}")
623	                    failed_targets.append(target)
624	                else:
625	                    logging.info(f"  PASS: {target}")
626	            except Exception as e:
627	                logging.error(f"  ERROR: {target} - {e}")
628	                failed_targets.append(target)
629	        else:
630	            logging.warning(f"  SKIP: {target} (ELF not found)")
631	
632	    logging.info("\n--- Suggested SPIKE_DENYLIST ---")
633	    logging.info("SPIKE_DENYLIST = [")
634	    for t in failed_targets:
635	        logging.info(f'    "{t}",')
636	    logging.info("]")
637	
638	
639	def run_full_regression(tests_to_run: List[Tuple[str, str]], spike_bin: str,
640	                        mpact_root: str, mpact_riscv_root: Optional[str],
641	                        temp_elf_dir: str, simulator: str):
642	    # Build the UVM simulator once
643	    if not build_simulator(mpact_root, simulator, mpact_riscv_root):
644	        logging.critical("ERROR: Simulator build failed. Aborting regression.")
645	        sys.exit(1)
646	
647	    # Setup output directory
648	    timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
649	    output_dir = f"uvm_regression_{timestamp}"
650	    logs_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "logs")
651	    os.makedirs(logs_dir, exist_ok=True)
652	    logging.info(f"Regression results will be stored in: {output_dir}")
653	
654	    # 1. Preparation: Copy ELFs and generate Spike logs
655	    logging.info(f"Preparing {len(tests_to_run)} tests...")
656	    test_info_map = {} # target -> info dict
657	    for i, (target, src_elf) in enumerate(tests_to_run):
658	        if src_elf and os.path.exists(src_elf):
659	            safe_name = target.replace('//', '').replace(':', '_').replace('/', '_') + ".elf"
660	            dest_elf = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir, safe_name)
661	
662	            try:
663	                if os.path.exists(dest_elf): os.remove(dest_elf)
664	                shutil.copy2(src_elf, dest_elf)
665	                os.chmod(dest_elf, stat.S_IRUSR | stat.S_IWUSR | stat.S_IXUSR | stat.S_IRGRP | stat.S_IXGRP | stat.S_IROTH | stat.S_IXOTH)
666	
667	                entry_point = get_entry_point(dest_elf)
668	                spike_log_path = "NONE"
669	                if spike_bin and target not in SPIKE_DENYLIST:
670	                    spike_log_name = safe_name + ".spike.log"
671	                    temp_spike_log = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir, spike_log_name)
672	                    if os.path.exists(temp_spike_log): os.remove(temp_spike_log)
673	                    cmd_spike = [
674	                        spike_bin, f"-m{get_spike_memory_map_str()}", f"--isa={SPIKE_ISA}",
675	                        "--misaligned", "-l", "--log-commits", f"--pc={entry_point}", dest_elf
676	                    ]
677	                    try:
678	                        with open(os.devnull, 'w') as devnull:
679	                            subprocess.run(cmd_spike, stdout=devnull, stderr=devnull, timeout=30)
680	                        if os.path.exists(temp_spike_log):
681	                            shutil.copy2(temp_spike_log, os.path.join(logs_dir, spike_log_name))
682	                            spike_log_path = os.path.abspath(temp_spike_log)
683	                    except: pass
684	
685	                tohost_addr = get_tohost_addr(dest_elf)
686	                if tohost_addr is None: tohost_addr = 0xFFFFFFFF
687	                timeout = TIMEOUT_MAP.get(target, 100000)
688	
689	                test_info_map[target] = {
690	                    "elf": os.path.abspath(dest_elf),
691	                    "tohost": tohost_addr,
692	                    "entry": entry_point,
693	                    "timeout": timeout,
694	                    "spike": spike_log_path,
695	                    "safe_log": safe_name.replace('.elf', '.log')
696	                }
697	            except Exception as e:
698	                logging.error(f"Failed to prepare {target}: {e}")
699	
700	    # 2. Execute Simulation with Crash Recovery
701	    results = []
702	    completed_targets = set()
703	    pending_targets = [t for t, _ in tests_to_run if t in test_info_map]
704	
705	    env = os.environ.copy()
706	    env["CORALNPU_MPACT"] = mpact_root
707	    regression_log_path = os.path.join(logs_dir, "regression.log")
708	
709	    logging.info("--- Starting Batch UVM Regression ---")
710	
711	    while pending_targets:
712	        # Create REGRESSION_LIST for current batch
713	        batch_list_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "current_batch.txt")
714	        with open(batch_list_path, 'w') as f_list:
715	            for t in pending_targets:
716	                info = test_info_map[t]
717	                f_list.write(f"{info['elf']} {info['tohost']:08x} {info['entry']:08x} {info['timeout']} {info['spike']} {t}\n")
718	
719	        cmd = [
720	            "make", "-C", "tests/uvm", "run", 
721	            "UVM_VERBOSITY=UVM_LOW",
722	            "UVM_TESTNAME=coralnpu_regression_test",
723	            f"SIMULATOR={simulator}",
724	            f"EXTRA_PLUSARGS=+REGRESSION_LIST={os.path.abspath(batch_list_path)}"
725	        ]
726	
727	        current_target = None
728	        current_test_log = None
729	        current_reason = "None"
730	
731	        try:
732	            with open(regression_log_path, 'a') as f_log:
733	                process = subprocess.Popen(cmd, env=env, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True)
734	
735	                for line in process.stdout:
736	                    f_log.write(line)
737	                    if current_test_log: current_test_log.write(line)
738	
739	                    # Detect start of test
740	                    start_match = re.search(r'--- STARTING TEST: (.*?) ---', line)
741	                    if start_match:
742	                        current_target = start_match.group(1)
743	                        logging.info(f"Running UVM for {current_target}...")
744	
745	                        log_path = os.path.join(logs_dir, test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
746	                        if current_test_log: current_test_log.close()
747	                        current_test_log = open(log_path, 'w')
748	                        current_test_log.write(line)
749	                        current_reason = "None"
750	                        continue
751	
752	                    # Parse for failure reasons
753	                    if current_target:
754	                        # Prioritize the first error found for the summary
755	                        err_match = re.search(r"^\s*(UVM_(?:FATAL|ERROR)(?!.*:\s+0\s*$).*)$", line, re.MULTILINE)
756	                        if err_match:
757	                            err_msg = err_match.group(1).strip()
758	                            logging.error(f"    {err_msg}")
759	                            if current_reason == "None":
760	                                current_reason = err_msg.replace(',', ';')
761	                        else:
762	                            # Match standard Verilog Error/Fatal/Assertion
763	                            verilog_err = re.search(r"^(Error|Fatal|Runtime Error|Assertion failed):? (.*)$", line, re.MULTILINE)
764	                            if verilog_err:
765	                                err_msg = verilog_err.group(0).strip()
766	                                logging.error(f"    {err_msg}")
767	                                if current_reason == "None":
768	                                    current_reason = err_msg.replace(',', ';')
769	
770	                        if "** UVM TEST PASSED **" in line:
771	                            logging.info(f"  Result: PASS - {current_reason}")
772	                            results.append({
773	                                "Target": current_target, "Status": "PASS", "Reason": current_reason,
774	                                "Log Path": os.path.join("logs", test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
775	                            })
776	                            completed_targets.add(current_target)
777	                            current_test_log.close()
778	                            current_test_log = None
779	                            current_target = None
780	                        elif "** UVM TEST FAILED **" in line:
781	                            logging.info(f"  Result: FAIL - {current_reason}")
782	                            results.append({
783	                                "Target": current_target, "Status": "FAIL", "Reason": current_reason,
784	                                "Log Path": os.path.join("logs", test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
785	                            })
786	                            completed_targets.add(current_target)
787	                            current_test_log.close()
788	                            current_test_log = None
789	                            current_target = None
790	
791	                process.wait()
792	                if current_test_log: current_test_log.close()
793	
794	                # If process ended while a test was running, mark it as a crash
795	                if current_target:
796	                    status = "FAIL"
797	                    if current_reason == "None":
798	                        current_reason = "Simulator exited early or crashed"
799	                    logging.error(f"  Result: {status} - {current_reason}")
800	                    results.append({
801	                        "Target": current_target, "Status": status, "Reason": current_reason,
802	                        "Log Path": os.path.join("logs", test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
803	                    })
804	                    completed_targets.add(current_target)
805	                    current_target = None
806	
807	        except Exception as e:
808	            logging.error(f"Batch execution failed: {e}")
809	            break # Avoid infinite loop if prepare/launch fails fundamentally
810	
811	        # Update pending targets for next batch attempt
812	        pending_targets = [t for t in pending_targets if t not in completed_targets]
813	        if pending_targets:
814	            logging.warning(f"Simulator crashed. Restarting for {len(pending_targets)} remaining tests...")
815	
816	    # 3. Finalization
817	    csv_file = os.path.join(output_dir, "uvm_results.csv")
818	    with open(csv_file, "w", newline='') as csvfile:
819	        fieldnames = ["Target", "Status", "Reason", "Log Path"]
820	        writer = csv.DictWriter(csvfile, fieldnames=fieldnames)
821	        writer.writeheader()
822	        for row in results:
823	            writer.writerow(row)
824	
825	    logging.info(f"Results written to {csv_file}")
826	    zip_filename = f"{output_dir}"
827	    logging.info(f"Creating archive {zip_filename}.zip...")
828	    shutil.make_archive(zip_filename, 'zip', output_dir)
829	    logging.info(f"Artifact created: {os.path.abspath(zip_filename + '.zip')}")
830	
831	    any_failed = any(r["Status"] != "PASS" for r in results)
832	    if any_failed:
833	        num_failed = sum(1 for r in results if r["Status"] != "PASS")
834	        logging.error(f"Regression FAILED: {num_failed} tests failed.")
835	        sys.exit(1)
836	
837	    logging.info("Regression PASSED.")
838	
839	
840	def main():
841	    args = parse_arguments()
842	    mpact_root, mpact_riscv_root = get_mpact_configs(args)
843	
844	    if args.mpact_commit:
845	        checkout_git_commit(mpact_root, args.mpact_commit)
846	
847	    if args.mpact_riscv_commit and mpact_riscv_root:
848	        checkout_git_commit(mpact_riscv_root, args.mpact_riscv_commit)
849	
850	    logging.info(f"Using MPACT root: {mpact_root}")
851	    if mpact_riscv_root:
852	        logging.info(f"Using MPACT RISCV root: {mpact_riscv_root}")
853	
854	    standard_targets = get_targets(args.limit, args.target)
855	
856	    if args.list_targets:
857	        logging.info("Targets to be verified:")
858	        for t in standard_targets:
859	            logging.info(t)
860	        logging.info(
861	            "(Note: riscv-tests are discovered after build and not listed here unless built)"
862	        )
863	        return
864	
865	    tests_to_run = prepare_tests(args, standard_targets)
866	    logging.info(f"Found {len(tests_to_run)} tests to run.")
867	
868	    # Build Spike once
869	    spike_bin = build_spike()
870	    if not spike_bin or not os.path.exists(spike_bin):
871	        logging.critical("ERROR: Spike binary not found. Aborting.")
872	        sys.exit(1)
873	
874	    # Use secure temporary directory
875	    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="uvm_reg_") as temp_elf_dir:
876	        logging.info(f"Using temp ELF directory: {temp_elf_dir}")
877	
878	        if args.check_spike_timeouts:
879	            run_spike_timeout_check(tests_to_run, spike_bin, temp_elf_dir)
880	            return
881	
882	        run_full_regression(tests_to_run, spike_bin, mpact_root,
883	                            mpact_riscv_root, temp_elf_dir, args.simulator)
884	
885	
886	if __name__ == "__main__":
887	    main()
888

import subprocess
import fnmatch
import os
import csv
import sys
import re
import argparse
import shutil
import stat
import datetime
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
import signal
import tempfile
import logging
from typing import List, Tuple, Optional, Dict
from elftools.elf.elffile import ELFFile

# Configure logging
logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO,
                    format="%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s",
                    datefmt="%H:%M:%S")

# List of targets to exclude from the regression
DENYLIST = [
    # Checks mcycle
    "//tests/cocotb/tutorial/counters:inst_cycle_counter_example",
    "//tests/cocotb/coralnpu_isa:perf_counters",
    # Peripherals
    "//tests/cocotb:timer_interrupt_test",
    "//tests/cocotb:plic_test",
    # RVV exceptions, not supported by MPACT (yet)
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv:vill_test",
    "//tests/cocotb:vector_store",
    "//tests/cocotb:vector_store_fault",
    # Jump to dtcm (also disabled in cocotb)
    "//third_party/riscv-tests:rv32ui-p-fence_i",
    "//third_party/riscv-tests:rv32ui-v-fence_i",
    # Actual RVV bugs?
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv:vmsif_test",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv:vmsbf_test",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/load_store:load_unit_masked",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/load_store:store_unit_masked",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vmsge_vx_test",
    # MPACT needs update to canonical-NaN
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:rvv_fdiv_float_rdn_m1",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:rvv_fdiv_float_rmm_m1",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:rvv_fdiv_float_rne_m1",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:rvv_fdiv_float_rtz_m1",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:rvv_fdiv_float_rup_m1",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfdiv_vf_test_rdn",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfdiv_vf_test_rmm",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfdiv_vf_test_rne",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfdiv_vf_test_rtz",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfdiv_vf_test_rup",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfrdiv_vf_test_rdn",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfrdiv_vf_test_rmm",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfrdiv_vf_test_rne",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfrdiv_vf_test_rtz",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/arithmetics:vfrdiv_vf_test_rup",
    # Exclude until MPACT supports the vector bf16 spec.
    "//tests/cocotb:zvfbf_test",
    # Exclude all ml_ops tests from regression
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_float_matmul",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_float_matmul_assembly",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_float_matmul_optimized",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul_assembly",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul_assembly_highmem",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul_assembly_itcm512kb_dtcm512kb",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul_highmem",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul_itcm512kb_dtcm512kb",
    # 1) UVM backdoor loader does not support loading to external memory (.extdata).
    # 2) UVM testbench lacks mechanism to initialize input/scale/zp data in external memory.
    "//internal/kernels:*",
    "//internal/kernels/*",
]

# List of targets to exclude from Spike co-simulation (e.g. tests requiring external IRQs)
SPIKE_DENYLIST = [
    "//hw_sim:mailbox_example",
    "//tests/cocotb/exceptions:store_fault_0",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv:rvv_add",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv:rvv_load",
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv:vstart_store",
    "//tests/cocotb:loop",
    "//tests/cocotb:registers",
    "//tests/cocotb:software_interrupt_test",
    "//tests/cocotb:stress_test",
    "//tests/cocotb:wfi_slot_0",
    "//tests/cocotb:wfi_slot_1",
    "//tests/cocotb:wfi_slot_2",
    "//tests/cocotb:wfi_slot_3",
]

# Map of targets to custom timeouts (in nanoseconds)
TIMEOUT_MAP = {
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul": 100000000,
    "//tests/cocotb/rvv/ml_ops:rvv_matmul_assembly": 100000000,
    "//examples:coralnpu_v2_rvv_add_intrinsic": 200000,
}

# Spike simulation parameters
SPIKE_MEMORY_REGIONS = [
    (0x0, 0x2000),  # ITCM
    (0x10000, 0x8000),  # DTCM
    (0x20000000, 0x400000),  # DRAM
]
SPIKE_ISA = "rv32imf_zve32f_zvl128b_zicsr_zifencei_zbb_zfbfmin_zvfbfa"


def get_spike_memory_map_str() -> str:

    return ",".join([
        f"0x{start:x}:0x{length:x}" for start, length in SPIKE_MEMORY_REGIONS
    ])


def get_targets(limit: Optional[int] = None,
                target: Optional[str] = None) -> List[str]:

    if target:
        return [target]

    logging.info("Querying bazel targets...")
    # Using --output=xml to parse attributes
    cmd = ["bazel", "query", "kind(coralnpu_v2_binary, //...)", "--output=xml"]
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, check=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(f"Bazel query failed: {e}")
        if e.stderr:
            logging.error(f"Stderr: {e.stderr}")
        sys.exit(1)
    root = ET.fromstring(result.stdout)
    targets = []
    for rule in root.findall('rule'):
        target_name_full = rule.attrib['name']  # //package:target

        # Check against DENYLIST
        if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(target_name_full, pattern) for pattern in DENYLIST):
            continue

        # Check linker_script attribute
        linker_script_elem = rule.find("label[@name='linker_script']")
        if linker_script_elem is not None:
            linker_script = linker_script_elem.attrib['value']

            # Extract package and name from target
            # e.g. //tests/cocotb:align_test
            if ':' in target_name_full:
                parts = target_name_full.split(':')
                pkg = parts[0]
                name = parts[1]
            else:
                # Handling edge case //package (implicit :package)
                pkg = target_name_full
                name = target_name_full.split('/')[-1]

            # Construct expected default linker script
            # Default is generated in the same package with name <target_name>.ld
            expected_linker_script = f"{pkg}:{name}.ld"

            if linker_script == expected_linker_script:
                targets.append(target_name_full)
            else:
                # Skipping targets with custom/non-default linker scripts
                pass
        else:
            # If no linker_script attribute is found, it might not be a valid target for us, skip.
            pass

    targets = sorted(targets)
    if limit:
        return targets[:limit]
    return targets


def build_targets(targets: List[str]) -> bool:
    logging.info(f"Building {len(targets)} targets...")
    # Split into chunks to avoid command line length limits if necessary,
    # but for now pass all. Bazel handles many targets well.
    cmd = ["bazel", "build"] + targets
    try:
        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
        return True
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(f"Build failed for some targets: {e}")
        return False


def get_elf_source_path(target: str) -> Optional[str]:
    # Use bazel cquery to get the actual output path
    cmd = ["bazel", "cquery", "--output=files", target]
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd,
                                capture_output=True,
                                text=True,
                                check=True)
        lines = result.stdout.strip().split('\n')
        for line in lines:
            if line.endswith('.elf'):
                return line.strip()
        return None
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(f"Error finding ELF source path: {e}")
        return None


def get_entry_point(elf_path: str) -> int:
    try:
        with open(elf_path, 'rb') as f:
            elf = ELFFile(f)
            return elf.header.e_entry
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Error reading ELF entry point: {e}")
        return 0


def get_tohost_addr(elf_path: str) -> Optional[int]:
    try:
        with open(elf_path, 'rb') as f:
            elf = ELFFile(f)
            symtab = elf.get_section_by_name('.symtab')
            if symtab:
                syms = symtab.get_symbol_by_name('tohost')
                if syms and len(syms) > 0:
                    return syms[0]['st_value']
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Error reading tohost addr: {e}")
    return None

def build_simulator(mpact_root: str,
                    simulator: str,
                    mpact_riscv_root: Optional[str] = None) -> bool:
    logging.info("Building UVM Simulator (simv)...")
    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["CORALNPU_MPACT"] = mpact_root
    if mpact_riscv_root:
        env["CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV"] = mpact_riscv_root

    cmd = ["make", "-C", "tests/uvm", "compile", f"SIMULATOR={simulator}"]
    try:
        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True, env=env)
        return True
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(f"Simulator build failed: {e}")
        return False


def build_spike() -> Optional[str]:
    logging.info("Building Spike Simulator...")
    cmd = ["bazel", "build", "@riscv_isa_sim//:riscv_isa_sim"]
    try:
        subprocess.run(cmd, check=True)
        # Return the absolute path to the binary
        return os.path.abspath(
            "bazel-bin/external/riscv_isa_sim/riscv_isa_sim/bin/spike")
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(f"Spike build failed: {e}")
        return None


def generate_spike_log(spike_bin: str,
                       elf_path: str,
                       log_path: str,
                       entry_point: int = 0,
                       timeout: int = 30) -> bool:

    logging.info(
        f"Generating Spike log for {elf_path} (Entry: 0x{entry_point:x})...")
    cmd = [
        spike_bin, f"-m{get_spike_memory_map_str()}", f"--isa={SPIKE_ISA}",
        "--misaligned", "-l", "--log-commits", f"--pc={entry_point}", elf_path
    ]
    try:
        with open(log_path, 'w') as f:
            process = subprocess.Popen(cmd,
                                       stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
                                       stdout=f,
                                       stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
                                       start_new_session=True)
            try:
                process.wait(timeout=timeout)
            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
                logging.warning(f"Spike timed out (PID: {process.pid})")
                os.killpg(os.getpgid(process.pid), signal.SIGTERM)
                process.wait(timeout=5)
                return False

            if process.returncode != 0:
                logging.error(
                    f"Spike failed with exit code {process.returncode}")
                return False
        return True
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Spike generation failed: {e}")
        return False


def run_uvm(elf_path: str,
            spike_log_path: Optional[str] = None,
            target: Optional[str] = None,
            mpact_root: Optional[str] = None,
            tohost_addr: Optional[int] = None) -> Tuple[str, str, str]:

    logging.info(f"Running UVM for {elf_path}...")
    if not mpact_root:
        mpact_root = resolve_default_mpact_root()
    if not os.path.exists(elf_path):
        # We don't want to deal too much with handling exceptions in
        # the run loop that calls this. We'll just log in the CSV and let
        # the user pick up the pieces.
        return "BUILD_ARTIFACT_MISSING", "ELF file not found", ""

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["CORALNPU_MPACT"] = mpact_root
    # Use absolute path for TEST_ELF
    abs_elf_path = os.path.abspath(elf_path)
    cmd = [
        "make", "-C", "tests/uvm", "run", "UVM_VERBOSITY=UVM_HIGH",
        f"TEST_ELF={abs_elf_path}"
    ]

    if target and target in TIMEOUT_MAP:
        timeout_ns = TIMEOUT_MAP[target]
        cmd.append(f"TEST_TIMEOUT_NS={timeout_ns}")
        logging.info(f"  Using custom timeout: {timeout_ns} ns")


    if tohost_addr is not None:
        cmd.append(f"EXTRA_PLUSARGS=+TOHOST_ADDR={tohost_addr:08x}")

    if spike_log_path:
        cmd.append(f"SPIKE_LOG={os.path.abspath(spike_log_path)}")

    max_retries = 3
    for attempt in range(1, max_retries + 1):
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(cmd,
                                    capture_output=True,
                                    text=True,
                                    env=env)
            output = result.stdout + result.stderr
            if result.returncode != 0 and "1-800-VERILOG" in output:
                if attempt < max_retries:
                    logging.warning(
                        f"  WARNING: License failure detected (1-800-VERILOG), retrying... (Attempt {attempt}/{max_retries})"
                    )
                    continue
                else:
                    logging.error(
                        f"  ERROR: License failure detected (1-800-VERILOG), max retries reached."
                    )
            # If successful or failed for other reasons, break loop
            break

        except Exception as e:
            return "EXEC_FAIL", str(e), ""

    # Process result (from last attempt)
    try:
        # Check for UVM errors/fatals regardless of return code
        # Match lines like: UVM_ERROR file.sv(123) @ 100: ... or UVM_FATAL @ 100: ...
        # Exclude summary lines like "UVM_ERROR : 0" or "Number of ... : 0"
        uvm_err = re.search(r"^\s*(UVM_(?:FATAL|ERROR)(?!.*:\s+0\s*$).*)$",
                            output, re.MULTILINE)

        if result.returncode != 0:
            status = "FAIL"
            reason = "Unknown Error"

            if uvm_err:
                reason = uvm_err.group(1).strip()
            elif "AXI_DECERR" in output:
                reason = "AXI_DECERR detected"
            else:
                lines = output.strip().split('\n')
                reason = "Make failed: " + (lines[-1] if lines else "Unknown")

            # Sanitize reason for CSV (remove commas and newlines)
            reason = reason.replace(',', ';').replace('\n', ' ')
            return status, reason, output

        # Even if return code is 0, check for UVM errors
        if uvm_err:
            reason = uvm_err.group(1).strip()
            # Sanitize reason for CSV
            reason = reason.replace(',', ';').replace('\n', ' ')
            return "FAIL", reason, output

        status = "PASS"
        return status, "None", output

    except Exception as e:
        return "EXEC_FAIL", str(e), ""


def get_riscv_test_artifacts() -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
    logging.info("Collecting riscv-tests artifacts...")
    target = "//third_party/riscv-tests:all_files"
    cmd = ["bazel", "cquery", target, "--output=files"]
    try:
        result = subprocess.run(cmd,
                                capture_output=True,
                                text=True,
                                check=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(f"Failed to query riscv-tests outputs: {e}")
        return []

    artifacts = []
    dirs = result.stdout.strip().split('\n')
    for d in dirs:
        d = d.strip()
        if not os.path.isdir(d):
            continue

        for root, _, files in os.walk(d):
            for f in files:
                if f.endswith('.dump'):
                    continue
                # heuristic: starts with rv32
                if f.startswith('rv32'):
                    full_path = os.path.join(root, f)
                    # Construct a pseudo-target name
                    # e.g. //third_party/riscv-tests:rv32ui-p-add
                    name = f
                    pseudo_target = f"//third_party/riscv-tests:{name}"
                    if any(fnmatch.fnmatch(pseudo_target, pattern) for pattern in DENYLIST):
                        continue
                    artifacts.append((pseudo_target, full_path))

    return sorted(artifacts)


def parse_arguments():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Run UVM regression")
    parser.add_argument("--limit", type=int, help="Limit number of tests")
    parser.add_argument("--target", type=str, help="Run a single target")
    parser.add_argument("--simulator",
                        type=str,
                        help="Defines simulator used for tests",
                        default="vcs")
    parser.add_argument("--list-targets",
                        action="store_true",
                        help="List targets and exit")
    parser.add_argument("--check-spike-timeouts",
                        action="store_true",
                        help="Run only Spike generation to identify timeouts")
    parser.add_argument("--skip-riscv-tests",
                        action="store_true",
                        help="Skip riscv-tests")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--mpact-root",
        type=str,
        help="Path to MPACT root directory (overrides CORALNPU_MPACT env var)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--mpact-riscv-root",
        type=str,
        help=
        "Path to MPACT RISCV directory (overrides CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV env var)"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--mpact-commit",
                        type=str,
                        help="Git commit hash to checkout for MPACT root")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--mpact-riscv-commit",
        type=str,
        help="Git commit hash to checkout for MPACT RISCV root")
    return parser.parse_args()


def checkout_git_commit(repo_root: str, commit: str):
    logging.info(f"Checking out {repo_root} at commit {commit}...")
    if not os.path.isdir(os.path.join(repo_root, ".git")):
        logging.error(
            f"{repo_root} is not a git repository. Cannot checkout commit.")
        sys.exit(1)

    try:
        # Fetch first to ensure we have the commit
        subprocess.run(["git", "-C", repo_root, "fetch"], check=True)
        subprocess.run(["git", "-C", repo_root, "checkout", commit],
                       check=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        logging.error(
            f"Failed to checkout commit {commit} in {repo_root}: {e}")
        sys.exit(1)


def resolve_default_mpact_root() -> str:
    # 1. Check if env var is already set
    if "CORALNPU_MPACT" in os.environ:
        return os.environ["CORALNPU_MPACT"]

    # 2. If not set, dynamically resolve using Bazel
    try:
        logging.info("Dynamically resolving default @coralnpu_mpact path via Bazel...")
        # Force Bazel to fetch the repository if needed
        bazel_cache = os.environ.get("BAZEL_CACHE")
        fetch_cmd = ["bazel", "build"]
        if bazel_cache:
            fetch_cmd.append(f"--repository_cache={bazel_cache}")
        fetch_cmd.extend(["@coralnpu_mpact//:coralnpu_sim_users"])

        # Run the build to force fetch
        subprocess.check_output(fetch_cmd, stderr=subprocess.DEVNULL)

        # Query bazel info output_base to get the dynamic cache location
        output_base = subprocess.check_output(["bazel", "info", "output_base"]).decode("utf-8").strip()
        resolved_path = os.path.join(output_base, "external", "coralnpu_mpact")
        logging.info(f"Resolved default @coralnpu_mpact path: {resolved_path}")
        return resolved_path
    except Exception as e:
        logging.critical(f"Failed to dynamically resolve @coralnpu_mpact path via Bazel: {e}.")
        sys.exit(1)


def get_mpact_configs(args) -> Tuple[str, Optional[str]]:
    if args.mpact_root:
        mpact_root = os.path.abspath(args.mpact_root)
    else:
        mpact_root = resolve_default_mpact_root()

    mpact_riscv_root = None
    if args.mpact_riscv_root:
        mpact_riscv_root = os.path.abspath(args.mpact_riscv_root)
    elif "CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV" in os.environ:
        mpact_riscv_root = os.environ["CORALNPU_MPACT_RISCV"]

    return mpact_root, mpact_riscv_root


def prepare_tests(args, standard_targets: List[str]) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
    # Build targets
    # Filter out pseudo-targets from standard_targets if they match riscv-tests pattern
    real_targets = [
        t for t in standard_targets
        if not t.startswith("//third_party/riscv-tests:")
    ]
    targets_to_build = real_targets
    if not args.skip_riscv_tests:
        targets_to_build.append("//third_party/riscv-tests:all_files")

    if not build_targets(targets_to_build):
        logging.warning(
            "WARNING: Some targets failed to build. Continuing with available artifacts."
        )

    # Now populate tests_to_run with valid ELFs
    tests_to_run = []

    # 1. RISC-V Tests
    if not args.skip_riscv_tests:
        riscv_tests = get_riscv_test_artifacts()
        for t, elf in riscv_tests:
            if args.target and args.target != t:
                continue
            tests_to_run.append((t, elf))

    # 2. Standard Targets
    if standard_targets:
        logging.info("Resolving standard target artifacts...")
    for t in standard_targets:
        if args.target and args.target != t:
            continue
        elf = get_elf_source_path(t)
        if elf:
            tests_to_run.append((t, elf))

    # Apply global limit if set
    if args.limit:
        tests_to_run = tests_to_run[:args.limit]

    return tests_to_run


def run_spike_timeout_check(tests_to_run: List[Tuple[str, str]],
                            spike_bin: str, temp_elf_dir: str):
    logging.info("--- Checking Spike Timeouts ---")
    failed_targets = []
    for i, (target, src_elf) in enumerate(tests_to_run):
        logging.info(f"[{i+1}/{len(tests_to_run)}] Checking {target}")

        if src_elf and os.path.exists(src_elf):
            safe_name = target.replace('//', '').replace(':', '_').replace(
                '/', '_') + ".elf"
            dest_elf = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir, safe_name)
            try:
                if os.path.exists(dest_elf): os.remove(dest_elf)
                shutil.copy2(src_elf, dest_elf)
                # Permissions: 755 / -rwxr-xr-x / u=rwx,go=rx
                os.chmod(
                    dest_elf, stat.S_IRUSR | stat.S_IWUSR | stat.S_IXUSR
                    | stat.S_IRGRP
                    | stat.S_IXGRP | stat.S_IROTH | stat.S_IXOTH)

                entry_point = get_entry_point(dest_elf)
                temp_spike_log = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir,
                                              safe_name + ".spike.log")

                if not generate_spike_log(
                        spike_bin,
                        dest_elf,
                        temp_spike_log,
                        entry_point,
                        timeout=10):  # Short timeout for check
                    logging.error(f"  FAIL: {target}")
                    failed_targets.append(target)
                else:
                    logging.info(f"  PASS: {target}")
            except Exception as e:
                logging.error(f"  ERROR: {target} - {e}")
                failed_targets.append(target)
        else:
            logging.warning(f"  SKIP: {target} (ELF not found)")

    logging.info("\n--- Suggested SPIKE_DENYLIST ---")
    logging.info("SPIKE_DENYLIST = [")
    for t in failed_targets:
        logging.info(f'    "{t}",')
    logging.info("]")


def run_full_regression(tests_to_run: List[Tuple[str, str]], spike_bin: str,
                        mpact_root: str, mpact_riscv_root: Optional[str],
                        temp_elf_dir: str, simulator: str):
    # Build the UVM simulator once
    if not build_simulator(mpact_root, simulator, mpact_riscv_root):
        logging.critical("ERROR: Simulator build failed. Aborting regression.")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Setup output directory
    timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().strftime("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
    output_dir = f"uvm_regression_{timestamp}"
    logs_dir = os.path.join(output_dir, "logs")
    os.makedirs(logs_dir, exist_ok=True)
    logging.info(f"Regression results will be stored in: {output_dir}")

    # 1. Preparation: Copy ELFs and generate Spike logs
    logging.info(f"Preparing {len(tests_to_run)} tests...")
    test_info_map = {} # target -> info dict
    for i, (target, src_elf) in enumerate(tests_to_run):
        if src_elf and os.path.exists(src_elf):
            safe_name = target.replace('//', '').replace(':', '_').replace('/', '_') + ".elf"
            dest_elf = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir, safe_name)

            try:
                if os.path.exists(dest_elf): os.remove(dest_elf)
                shutil.copy2(src_elf, dest_elf)
                os.chmod(dest_elf, stat.S_IRUSR | stat.S_IWUSR | stat.S_IXUSR | stat.S_IRGRP | stat.S_IXGRP | stat.S_IROTH | stat.S_IXOTH)

                entry_point = get_entry_point(dest_elf)
                spike_log_path = "NONE"
                if spike_bin and target not in SPIKE_DENYLIST:
                    spike_log_name = safe_name + ".spike.log"
                    temp_spike_log = os.path.join(temp_elf_dir, spike_log_name)
                    if os.path.exists(temp_spike_log): os.remove(temp_spike_log)
                    cmd_spike = [
                        spike_bin, f"-m{get_spike_memory_map_str()}", f"--isa={SPIKE_ISA}",
                        "--misaligned", "-l", "--log-commits", f"--pc={entry_point}", dest_elf
                    ]
                    try:
                        with open(os.devnull, 'w') as devnull:
                            subprocess.run(cmd_spike, stdout=devnull, stderr=devnull, timeout=30)
                        if os.path.exists(temp_spike_log):
                            shutil.copy2(temp_spike_log, os.path.join(logs_dir, spike_log_name))
                            spike_log_path = os.path.abspath(temp_spike_log)
                    except: pass

                tohost_addr = get_tohost_addr(dest_elf)
                if tohost_addr is None: tohost_addr = 0xFFFFFFFF
                timeout = TIMEOUT_MAP.get(target, 100000)

                test_info_map[target] = {
                    "elf": os.path.abspath(dest_elf),
                    "tohost": tohost_addr,
                    "entry": entry_point,
                    "timeout": timeout,
                    "spike": spike_log_path,
                    "safe_log": safe_name.replace('.elf', '.log')
                }
            except Exception as e:
                logging.error(f"Failed to prepare {target}: {e}")

    # 2. Execute Simulation with Crash Recovery
    results = []
    completed_targets = set()
    pending_targets = [t for t, _ in tests_to_run if t in test_info_map]

    env = os.environ.copy()
    env["CORALNPU_MPACT"] = mpact_root
    regression_log_path = os.path.join(logs_dir, "regression.log")

    logging.info("--- Starting Batch UVM Regression ---")

    while pending_targets:
        # Create REGRESSION_LIST for current batch
        batch_list_path = os.path.join(output_dir, "current_batch.txt")
        with open(batch_list_path, 'w') as f_list:
            for t in pending_targets:
                info = test_info_map[t]
                f_list.write(f"{info['elf']} {info['tohost']:08x} {info['entry']:08x} {info['timeout']} {info['spike']} {t}\n")

        cmd = [
            "make", "-C", "tests/uvm", "run", 
            "UVM_VERBOSITY=UVM_LOW",
            "UVM_TESTNAME=coralnpu_regression_test",
            f"SIMULATOR={simulator}",
            f"EXTRA_PLUSARGS=+REGRESSION_LIST={os.path.abspath(batch_list_path)}"
        ]

        current_target = None
        current_test_log = None
        current_reason = "None"

        try:
            with open(regression_log_path, 'a') as f_log:
                process = subprocess.Popen(cmd, env=env, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.STDOUT, text=True)

                for line in process.stdout:
                    f_log.write(line)
                    if current_test_log: current_test_log.write(line)

                    # Detect start of test
                    start_match = re.search(r'--- STARTING TEST: (.*?) ---', line)
                    if start_match:
                        current_target = start_match.group(1)
                        logging.info(f"Running UVM for {current_target}...")

                        log_path = os.path.join(logs_dir, test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
                        if current_test_log: current_test_log.close()
                        current_test_log = open(log_path, 'w')
                        current_test_log.write(line)
                        current_reason = "None"
                        continue

                    # Parse for failure reasons
                    if current_target:
                        # Prioritize the first error found for the summary
                        err_match = re.search(r"^\s*(UVM_(?:FATAL|ERROR)(?!.*:\s+0\s*$).*)$", line, re.MULTILINE)
                        if err_match:
                            err_msg = err_match.group(1).strip()
                            logging.error(f"    {err_msg}")
                            if current_reason == "None":
                                current_reason = err_msg.replace(',', ';')
                        else:
                            # Match standard Verilog Error/Fatal/Assertion
                            verilog_err = re.search(r"^(Error|Fatal|Runtime Error|Assertion failed):? (.*)$", line, re.MULTILINE)
                            if verilog_err:
                                err_msg = verilog_err.group(0).strip()
                                logging.error(f"    {err_msg}")
                                if current_reason == "None":
                                    current_reason = err_msg.replace(',', ';')

                        if "** UVM TEST PASSED **" in line:
                            logging.info(f"  Result: PASS - {current_reason}")
                            results.append({
                                "Target": current_target, "Status": "PASS", "Reason": current_reason,
                                "Log Path": os.path.join("logs", test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
                            })
                            completed_targets.add(current_target)
                            current_test_log.close()
                            current_test_log = None
                            current_target = None
                        elif "** UVM TEST FAILED **" in line:
                            logging.info(f"  Result: FAIL - {current_reason}")
                            results.append({
                                "Target": current_target, "Status": "FAIL", "Reason": current_reason,
                                "Log Path": os.path.join("logs", test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
                            })
                            completed_targets.add(current_target)
                            current_test_log.close()
                            current_test_log = None
                            current_target = None

                process.wait()
                if current_test_log: current_test_log.close()

                # If process ended while a test was running, mark it as a crash
                if current_target:
                    status = "FAIL"
                    if current_reason == "None":
                        current_reason = "Simulator exited early or crashed"
                    logging.error(f"  Result: {status} - {current_reason}")
                    results.append({
                        "Target": current_target, "Status": status, "Reason": current_reason,
                        "Log Path": os.path.join("logs", test_info_map[current_target]['safe_log'])
                    })
                    completed_targets.add(current_target)
                    current_target = None

        except Exception as e:
            logging.error(f"Batch execution failed: {e}")
            break # Avoid infinite loop if prepare/launch fails fundamentally

        # Update pending targets for next batch attempt
        pending_targets = [t for t in pending_targets if t not in completed_targets]
        if pending_targets:
            logging.warning(f"Simulator crashed. Restarting for {len(pending_targets)} remaining tests...")

    # 3. Finalization
    csv_file = os.path.join(output_dir, "uvm_results.csv")
    with open(csv_file, "w", newline='') as csvfile:
        fieldnames = ["Target", "Status", "Reason", "Log Path"]
        writer = csv.DictWriter(csvfile, fieldnames=fieldnames)
        writer.writeheader()
        for row in results:
            writer.writerow(row)

    logging.info(f"Results written to {csv_file}")
    zip_filename = f"{output_dir}"
    logging.info(f"Creating archive {zip_filename}.zip...")
    shutil.make_archive(zip_filename, 'zip', output_dir)
    logging.info(f"Artifact created: {os.path.abspath(zip_filename + '.zip')}")

    any_failed = any(r["Status"] != "PASS" for r in results)
    if any_failed:
        num_failed = sum(1 for r in results if r["Status"] != "PASS")
        logging.error(f"Regression FAILED: {num_failed} tests failed.")
        sys.exit(1)

    logging.info("Regression PASSED.")


def main():
    args = parse_arguments()
    mpact_root, mpact_riscv_root = get_mpact_configs(args)

    if args.mpact_commit:
        checkout_git_commit(mpact_root, args.mpact_commit)

    if args.mpact_riscv_commit and mpact_riscv_root:
        checkout_git_commit(mpact_riscv_root, args.mpact_riscv_commit)

    logging.info(f"Using MPACT root: {mpact_root}")
    if mpact_riscv_root:
        logging.info(f"Using MPACT RISCV root: {mpact_riscv_root}")

    standard_targets = get_targets(args.limit, args.target)

    if args.list_targets:
        logging.info("Targets to be verified:")
        for t in standard_targets:
            logging.info(t)
        logging.info(
            "(Note: riscv-tests are discovered after build and not listed here unless built)"
        )
        return

    tests_to_run = prepare_tests(args, standard_targets)
    logging.info(f"Found {len(tests_to_run)} tests to run.")

    # Build Spike once
    spike_bin = build_spike()
    if not spike_bin or not os.path.exists(spike_bin):
        logging.critical("ERROR: Spike binary not found. Aborting.")
        sys.exit(1)

    # Use secure temporary directory
    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix="uvm_reg_") as temp_elf_dir:
        logging.info(f"Using temp ELF directory: {temp_elf_dir}")

        if args.check_spike_timeouts:
            run_spike_timeout_check(tests_to_run, spike_bin, temp_elf_dir)
            return

        run_full_regression(tests_to_run, spike_bin, mpact_root,
                            mpact_riscv_root, temp_elf_dir, args.simulator)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

I now have a complete and thorough understanding of the VCS/UVM verification infrastructure. Let me summarize the key facts before writing the lecture:

## Key facts learned:

### 1. VCS support (doc/simulation.md)
- VCS support requires env vars: `VCS_HOME`, `LM_LICENSE_FILE`, `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`, `PATH`
- Two Bazel rules in `rules/vcs.bzl`: `vcs_testbench_test` and `vcs_binary`
- Default VCS disabled; use `bazel {build,run,test} --config=vcs` to enable
- CCACHE troubleshooting: `bazel --action_env=CCACHE_DISABLE=1 test --config=vcs //...`

### 2. .bazelrc --config=vcs mechanism
- Default: `build --build_tag_filters="-vcs,-synthesis,-power"` excludes vcs targets (most workstations lack licenses)
- `build:vcs --build_tag_filters="vcs"` inverts the filter to ONLY include vcs targets
- The `vcs_testbench_test` rule auto-adds `tags = ["vcs"]`, so it's excluded by default
- Various env vars passed through: VCS_HOME, LM_LICENSE_FILE, etc.

### 3. rules/vcs.bzl
- `_vcs_testbench_test`: simple rule, collects verilog files from deps, runs `vcs -full64 -sverilog`, produces simv. Used for unit-level SV testbenches (e.g. aligner_tb, multififo_tb)
- `_vcs_binary`: more sophisticated, handles C++ deps (MPACT cosim), objcopy strips .sframe, generates a runner script that translates `--binary=`, `--cycles=`, `--trace` args into VCS plusargs. Used for full-chip simulators via template_rule (8 variants in tests/vcs_sim/BUILD)
- Both wrap rules auto-tag with "vcs"

### 4. Two distinct UVM testbenches:

**A. tests/uvm/** — full-chip UVM testbench for `RvvCoreMiniVerificationAxi` DUT
- Driven by Makefile (Make + VCS, with Verilator alternative via SIMULATOR=verilator)
- DUT: RvvCoreMiniVerificationAxi (the verification variant exposing RVVI)
- Components: AXI master agent (TB drives DUT slave port), AXI slave agent (TB responds to DUT master port), IRQ agent, RVVI agent (passive), cosim checker (MPACT + Spike)
- env (coralnpu_env_pkg.sv): holds 5 agents/checkers
- tb/coralnpu_tb_top.sv: instantiates DUT + interfaces, clock/reset gen, ELF backdoor load via tohost monitoring, config_db sets
- Driven by Python: utils/run_uvm_regression.py queries bazel for coralnpu_v2_binary targets, builds ELFs, runs make run with each ELF, compares against MPACT cosim and optionally Spike (3-way)
- MPACT integration: builds coralnpu_cosim_lib_static, linked into simv
- The regression test is `coralnpu_regression_test` (batch via +REGRESSION_LIST)
- Uses DPI backdoor (sram_backdoor.cc) for fast ELF load
- Compares DUT retire (via RVVI) against MPACT reference model

**B. hdl/verilog/rvv/sve/rvv_backend_tb/** — block-level UVM testbench for the RVV backend (`rvv_backend` DUT) only
- Driven by its own Makefile with VCS
- DUT: rvv_backend (just the vector/matrix backend, not the whole core)
- env (rvv_backend_env.sv): rvs_agent + lsu_agent + scoreboard + coverage + behavior_model (mdl)
- rvs_agent: drives the RVV backend command interface (rvs_interface) — sequencer, driver, monitor + vrf_monitor
- lsu_agent: drives LSU interface
- rvv_scoreboard: compares DUT retire (VRF/XRF/vxsat/trap) against the behavior model (rvv_behavior_model = software reference of RVV ISA) — three checkers: rt_checker, vrf_checker, mem_access_checker

- rvv_behavior_model: the reference model (mdl), written in SV, implements RVV instruction semantics
- Tests: huge test library — alu_smoke_test, alu_vaddsub_test, ... etc. Each runs directed + random sequences
- regress.list (directed), regress_random.list (random), regress_corner.list (corner cases like div_zero, reset, large_vstart)
- Two test styles: directed (run_inst_iter — iterate over operand combinations) and random (run_inst_rand / alu_random_seq)
- Many scfg configs: rvv_backend (i3rp6), i2rp4, i2rp6, i3rp6, bringup — different issue width / read port configs

### 5. CI: .github/workflows/sim-verilator.yml
- Runs the UVM regression under Verilator (not VCS, since CI lacks VCS license)
- Clones uvm-verilator (UVM 1.2 port), coralnpu-mpact
- Runs `python3 utils/run_uvm_regression.py --simulator=verilator`
- This is the "UVM regression Verilator flow" from PR #97

- **Corner**: regress_corner.list — div_zero, reset, large_vstart, overflow
